selfEditingRules: Keep this file intent-focused and describe user-visible behavior and constraints rather than implementation details.
Update features and scenarios whenever the implemented behavior changes and the new behavior is intended.
Add, merge, split, or remove features and scenarios to keep the spec aligned with the current intended product behavior.
Prefer adjusting existing scenarios over adding redundant ones when intent can be expressed more clearly by rewriting.
If code and tests conflict with this file, first determine whether the implementation is intentionally different; if so, update this file to match the intended behavior.
Do not change testCommand unless explicitly instructed.

when: the user executes the template program without enabling debug mode
then: the program prints a Hello World message

scenarios.scenario: Developer runs the program in debug mode
when: the program is executed with DEBUG=1
then: the program runs its built-in sanity check
the program reports a passing test result instead of the normal greeting
